title: Miami International Autodrome
sub: A purpose-built ribbon wrapped around the Hard Rock Stadium: three long straights for slipstreaming, a tight technical infield, and a flyover that adds the lap’s only real elevation.

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: Long straight into Turn 1 braking, then quick direction changes through the fast opening complex.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: High-speed running with the second long straight into the heavy Turn 11 stop.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: The tight, technical Turn 13-16 infield over the highway flyover, then back to the line.

tyreAlloc: Softest in the range · C3 / C4 / C5
stratNote: Surface has matured since the 2023 resurface; grip builds fast and degradation is low. Track evolution is huge, so late runners benefit.
spots: End of the long straight from the final corner + DRS. Primary passing zone.
Heavy braking at the end of the second long straight + DRS.
Slow corner after the third straight; late-brake opportunity.
otNote: Three long straights with DRS give clear chances, but the twisty Turn 13-16 infield kills following and can bottleneck the field.
corners: Hard braking off the start straight; main overtaking and Turn 1 chaos.
Quick left-right transition out of Turn 1.
Tight chicane breaking up the opening flow.
Fast right onto a flowing stretch.
High-speed changes before the long straight.
Big stop at the end of the second straight + DRS. Strong zone.
Tight, low-speed switchbacks over the flyover. Slowest sequence.
Heavy braking after the third straight; secondary chance.
Final corners launching onto the main straight and DRS 1.
geo: Wrapped around the Hard Rock Stadium on a mix of public roads and parking lots. Purpose-built by Apex Circuit Design for 2022.
Sectors 1 and 3 fast and flowing with long straights; the Turn 13-16 infield is tight and technical.
High ambient and track temps stress tyres, and the late slot brings a real thunderstorm threat.
Long DRS straights reward low drag, but the infield wants downforce, so teams gamble on wing level.
